case_meta.name: ami-es2004a-5min
case_meta.description:
AMI Meeting Corpus, ES2004a, mid-meeting 5-minute window (audio offset
360-660s of the 17.5-min source). Real 4-person scripted-scenario meeting
recorded at IDIAP / University of Edinburgh. THIS is the canonical
academic diarization stress test — used by every major paper on speaker
diarization (pyannote, NeMo, x-vector, EEND, end-to-end neural diarization).
Numbers are directly comparable to the published literature.

Why this window matters:
- **All 4 speakers active** (FEE013, FEE016, MEE014, MEO015 — 2 female, 2 male)
- **94% speech density** — almost no silence to game DER on
- **17% overlap** (where 2+ speakers talk simultaneously) — the failure
  mode SCOTUS doesn't have, the one Keanan flagged as critical
- Mix-Headset audio = mixed beamform from individual lapel mics, similar
  acoustic conditions to a courtroom with multiple personal mics
case_meta.license: CC-BY 4.0 (AMI corpus). RTTM annotations: same. Fully redistributable.

ground_truth.notes:
RTTM trimmed to [360, 660]s of the source audio, then re-zeroed so the
case audio starts at t=0. Speaker labels preserved from the AMI standard
(FEE013/FEE016 = female participants, MEE014/MEO015 = male participants).
